title: populus nigra var. caudina
intro: Populus nigra var. caudina, also known as the Black Cottonwood, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its distinctive black bark and cotton-like seeds. The medicinal properties of Populus nigra var. caudina include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can be attributed to the presence of salicin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Populus nigra var. caudina can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments, including colds and flu.
